behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: evaluate-stage logic calling artifact persistence with the correct slot name. `stores grounding into a dedicated grounding artifact, not the plan artifact` describes a write-side effect across components, not just a pure local function, so Unit is too low. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; an in-process artifact store wiring test can expose wrong-slot writes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here is artifact target selection, not model output quality or multi-agentic decision chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Wire evaluate-stage command to a real in-process artifact repository used by the app (no mocks for slot mapping). Execute evaluate with sample grounding input and assert: `grounding` artifact is created/updated, `plan` artifact is unchanged by evaluate, and stored payload/type metadata match grounding semantics.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is cross-module read/write orchestration: read one artifact (`grounding`) and write another (`plan`). Not Unit because failure appears when implement flow and artifact store interact. Rejected System: no behavior text about real HTTP/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic despite LLM-involved feature area because the quoted requirement is infrastructure routing (`reads grounding ... writes ... to plan`), not judging model output quality; deterministic fixture for implement output content is acceptable here.
behaviors.test_description: Set up run context with preexisting `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage flow against real in-process artifact store, and assert implement consumed grounding context (e.g., passed into builder/input state) while final write target is `plan` only for execution output. Verify grounding artifact remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The failure is an unintended side effect at module boundary (deep-dive stage invoking artifact writes). `no longer overwrites plan as a redundant side effect` is not purely local logic; it needs stage+store wiring. Rejected Unit for that reason. Rejected System because no real infra requirement is stated. Rejected Agentic: behavior text anchors on suppression of `plan` overwrite when diagnosis is already present, which can be validated deterministically via issue fixture plus artifact state checks.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize issue content fixture containing diagnosis and preexisting `plan` artifact. Run deep-dive stage in-process with real artifact repository wiring. Assert deep-dive does not mutate `plan` (version/hash unchanged) and emits no redundant plan-write event.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets state handoff between producer and consumer modules within one run context. Not Unit because lineage bugs emerge when storage, run-context identity, and implement reader interact. Rejected System: no explicit OS/network/subprocess dependency in `same run context`. Rejected Agentic/Workflow under integration-brake (d): this is lineage plumbing correctness; no quoted requirement that model judgment determines handoff validity.
behaviors.test_description: Within one in-process run context, execute evaluate-stage write of grounding, then execute implement-stage read. Assert implement resolves artifact by run-context lineage (same run id/thread) and receives exactly the earlier grounding payload.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Quoted behavior explicitly requires `full multi-step agent run (deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement)` and outcome dependence across steps (`final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier`). That is sequential multi-step agent pipeline validation, so Workflow is the minimum. Rejected adjacent lower Agentic because single-step agentic execution cannot prove cross-step carryover through the full chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real three-stage chain end-to-end in one run context using production orchestration. Seed issue where deep-dive/evaluate produce distinctive grounding facts, then assert final implement plan includes those grounding facts and preserves traceable linkage to earlier artifact lineage.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration with storage side effects: missing-read path plus safe write behavior across artifacts. Not Unit because `without corrupting other stored artifacts` requires observing repository state interactions. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required. Rejected Agentic because requirement is safety/integrity of artifact operations under absent grounding, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` artifact in the run context against real in-process artifact repository containing other unrelated artifacts. Assert implement succeeds, writes a syntactically valid `plan` artifact, and leaves non-plan artifacts byte-for-byte unchanged.
